title: Improve error message when the the podman service is not enabled
body:
Currently if server is not connected, we return an error message that
is confusing users on Mac and Windows boxes.  The hope here is to make
it a little easier to discover that a Podman service is required.

This message is similar to what Docker puts out so people might under
stand it better.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

Works for me.

podman-remote
`Error: Cannot connect to the Podman socket, make sure there is a Podman REST API service running.: Get "http://d/v2.0.0/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory`

Similar enough to:

docker ([rootless](https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/rootless/#client))
`Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///run/user/1000/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?`
